findings.title: `total_input_tokens` now undercounts older `modelUsage` payloads that omit per-model cache fields
findings.description: The new branch switches to `modelUsage` whenever it exists, but only reads camelCase per-model cache counters. Older/result payloads can include `modelUsage` with `inputTokens` and `contextWindow` while keeping cache counts only in top-level `usage` (the previous test covered exactly that shape before being rewritten). In that case this code returns only `inputTokens` and drops top-level cache usage entirely, so the Discord `ctx:%` suffix becomes much lower than the real prompt footprint.
findings.suggestion: Keep the top-level `usage` values as the source of truth for `total_input_tokens`, or fall back per field: use per-model `cacheCreationInputTokens`/`cacheReadInputTokens` only when present and otherwise keep the top-level `usage` cache counters. Add a regression test for a mixed-format payload where `modelUsage` exists but lacks per-model cache fields while top-level `usage` still has them.

findings.title: Field semantics changed from aggregate tokens to one selected model's tokens
findings.description: Before this change, `total_input_tokens` was the aggregate `usage.input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens`. After the change it becomes the token total for the single `modelUsage` entry with the largest `contextWindow`. Downstream code still treats `total_input_tokens` as a total and formats it alongside aggregate `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` in Discord summaries. For multi-model turns this mixes aggregate token counts with a per-model context percentage, which is misleading and can hide heavy usage on the other model.
findings.suggestion: Either preserve `total_input_tokens` as the aggregate total from top-level `usage`, or introduce a separate field such as `main_model_input_tokens` for the context-window percentage calculation so the naming and downstream behavior stay consistent.

summary: The patch improves model selection for `context_window`, but it also changes `total_input_tokens` semantics in a way that undercounts some payloads and makes Discord context usage reporting inconsistent for multi-model turns.
